title: UK raises terrorism threat level to 'severe' after London stabbing and rising extremism
summary: MI5 raised the UK terrorism threat level from 'substantial' to 'severe,' signaling an attack within six months appears highly likely, driven by rising Islamist and extreme right-wing threats targeting Jewish and Israeli institutions.
contents:
## UK Terrorism Threat Level Raised to Severe

### Threat Elevation
The UK's domestic intelligence agency MI5 announced on May 2, 2026, that the Joint Terrorism Analysis Centre raised the national terrorism threat level from "substantial" to "severe" — the second-highest level, indicating an attack within six months appears "highly likely."

### Driving Factors
MI5 attributed the increase to "a rise in both Islamist and Extreme Right-Wing terrorist threat from individuals and small groups in the UK." The agency noted particular threats to "Jewish and Israeli individuals and institutions, in the context of the conflict in the Middle East."

### Recent Incidents
The elevation followed the stabbing of two Jewish men in the Golders Green area of London on Wednesday, though MI5 emphasized the decision reflected broader trends rather than that single attack. Other recent incidents included targeting of the Finchley Reform Synagogue and the Kenton United Synagogue in Harrow.

### US Embassy Response
The US embassy in London issued a security alert advising American citizens to remain "alert in public places," avoid schools, churches, tourist locations, and transportation hubs, and vary travel routes to reduce predictability. The advisory represented the second US embassy security notice in the UK within recent weeks.
